title: Relocate subagent-only skill templates into templates/skills and bundle them
description: Create the new `templates/skills/` source tree for subagent-only Codex skills, leave `memory` and `spec-management` in repo-root `.agents/skills/`, and update packaged asset helpers/package data so bundled subagent skill templates resolve from the new location.
acceptance_criteria: All subagent-only Codex skills previously authored under repo-root `.agents/skills/` are moved to `templates/skills/` with the same IDs and content shape.
Repo-root `.agents/skills/` retains only operator-facing exceptions, specifically `memory` and the existing `spec-management` skill in place.
Runtime asset helpers and package-data wiring expose the bundled subagent catalog from `templates/skills/` rather than the old `.agents/skills/` bundle root.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `templates/skills/**` with the moved subagent skill content, added bundled package copies under `src/agent_takt/_data/templates/skills/**`, updated `src/agent_takt/_assets.py` with a dedicated packaged skill-template helper, updated `src/agent_takt/skills.py` so runtime resolution prefers `templates/skills` and falls back to `.agents/skills` for exceptions, and updated `pyproject.toml` package-data to include `_data/templates/skills/**/*`. Also removed the packaged subagent-only files from `src/agent_takt/_data/agents_skills/**`, leaving the packaged `.agents` root for exception content. Syntax-only validation passed via `uv run python -m compileall src/agent_takt`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Physically remove the legacy subagent-only files still present under repo-root `.agents/skills/{capability,core,role,task}/**` so the source tree itself retains only `memory` and `skill-spec-management`.
handoff_summary.risks: Runtime behavior now prefers `templates/skills`, so execution should use the relocated catalog, but the undeleted legacy `.agents/skills` files still exist on disk and may confuse maintainers or any code path that inspects the repository tree directly instead of using `skills._skill_path()`.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run the same relocation cleanup in a workspace where repo-root `.agents/skills/**` is writable, deleting the legacy subagent-only files so only `memory` and `skill-spec-management` remain there.
handoff_summary.block_reason: The sandbox allows writes in normal project paths but rejects any create/delete operation under repo-root `.agents/skills/**` with `Operation not permitted`, so I could not complete the required source-tree cleanup there.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Avoid touching `templates/skills/**`, `src/agent_takt/_data/templates/skills/**`, and `src/agent_takt/_data/agents_skills/**` in parallel with the follow-up cleanup. The remaining `.agents/skills/**` deletion should be done with care because runtime now intentionally depends on fallback semantics for `memory` and `skill-spec-management`.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: I introduced a dedicated bundled `templates/skills` helper instead of repointing the existing `.agents` helper so runtime subagent resolution can move cleanly without breaking the operator-facing `.agents` exception catalog. In `skills._skill_path()`, I made `templates/skills` higher priority than repo-root `.agents/skills` so the relocated source of truth is used immediately even though the sandbox prevented deleting the stale `.agents` copies.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify that `prepare_isolated_execution_root()` loads Codex subagent skills from `templates/skills` in a source checkout and from bundled `_data/templates/skills` when project-local templates are absent, while `memory` still resolves from `.agents/skills`/bundled `.agents` fallback. Also verify packaging/install contexts still include `_data/templates/skills/**/*` and that no runtime path still expects bundled subagent skills under `_data/agents_skills`.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Repo-root `.agents/skills/**` could not be modified in this environment, so the repository source tree still contains the old subagent-only skill files even though runtime and bundled assets now use the relocated template catalog.

conflict_risks: Avoid touching `templates/skills/**`, `src/agent_takt/_data/templates/skills/**`, and `src/agent_takt/_data/agents_skills/**` in parallel with the follow-up cleanup. The remaining `.agents/skills/**` deletion should be done with care because runtime now intentionally depends on fallback semantics for `memory` and `skill-spec-management`.

# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.

Execution context:
You are running inside a shared feature worktree. Sibling sub-beads may also run in this same feature tree, but only when dependencies and file-scope claims allow it. Stay within this bead's scope.

Structured output requirements:
- always set `verdict` to `approved` or `needs_changes`.
- Always set `findings_count`; use `0` when there are no unresolved findings in this pass.
- Set `requires_followup` explicitly.
- Use `approved` when this bead is complete without follow-up; use `needs_changes` when blocking or handing off unresolved work.
